title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Lady Bunty and Miss Marple discuss the upcoming arrival of film star Marina Gregg at Gossington Hall. Lady Bunty notes that she learned about Gregg’s private life through magazines at a hair salon, though she expresses skepticism regarding rumors of Gregg being an alcoholic or having a mental breakdown. Miss Marple observes that the house has undergone extensive renovations, including the addition of six bathrooms, a Palm House, and a swimming pool, as well as the conversion of the late Arthur Bantry’s study and library into a music room. Lady Bunty remarks that her late husband, who was tone-deaf, would have hated the change.

During their conversation, Mrs. Bantry suggests that Miss Marple acts like a criminologist, a claim Miss Marple denies, attributing her insights instead to an understanding of human nature gained from living in a small village. Miss Marple mentions her interest in the local "new development" and notes that Miss Knight’s wages were paid by her nephew, Raymond.

After Miss Marple departs via the "Inch" car rental service, she visits Miss Knight. Miss Knight informs her that Dr. Huddlestone called and will visit the following morning. The next morning, Dr. Huddlestone arrives and drinks a glass of sherry offered by Miss Marple. He reprimands Miss Marple for her recent fall and mentions that she has been refusing to see Mr. Sandsford, a former partner of the doctor.
